brief.product_description: TrailForge is a modular hiking backpack built around a swappable frame system. The base 40L shell accepts clip-in modules: a hydration bladder sleeve, a camera cube with padded dividers, a solar-panel lid, a detachable summit daypack, and a bear-canister cradle. The suspension uses a load-lifting aluminium stay and a ventilated mesh back panel that keeps the pack 15mm off your spine for airflow on hot climbs. Every seam is taped, the main fabric is recycled 420D ripstop with a PFAS-free DWR finish, and the hip belt has two zippered pockets sized for a large phone. It is designed for weekend backpackers who hate owning five different packs for five different trips and want one system that reconfigures in under two minutes.
brief.target_audience: weekend backpackers and thru-hikers who own too much gear
brief.goal: drive pre-orders and newsletter signups before the spring launch

notes: Large channel list (within the 25 cap) with duplicates and odd casing.
